Mezzo-Soprano · Porto, Portugal

Mezzo-Soprano · Porto, Portugal

Paula Morna DÓRIA

Mezzo-Soprano — International Career

Mezzo-Soprano — Carreira Internacional

From Porto to Carnegie Hall, from Lisbon's CCB to the concert halls of New York — a voice of rare depth and dramatic intensity.

Do Porto ao Carnegie Hall, do CCB de Lisboa às salas de concerto de Nova Iorque — uma voz de rara profundidade e intensidade dramática.

Paula Morna DÓRIA is one of Portugal's most distinguished mezzo-sopranos, with an international career spanning opera, oratorio and concert. Born in Porto, she has performed at the CCB, Gulbenkian, Teatro Nacional de São Carlos, and Avery Fisher Hall at Lincoln Center, New York.

Her formation took place at ESMAE Porto, Indiana University Jacobs School of Music — under Virginia Zeani and Klara Barlow — and at the Mason Gross School of the Arts, Rutgers University, where she earned her DMA in Vocal Performance.

Paula Morna DÓRIA é uma das mezzo-sopranos mais distintas de Portugal, com carreira internacional em ópera, oratória e concerto. Natural do Porto, actuou no CCB, Gulbenkian, TNSC e no Avery Fisher Hall do Lincoln Center em Nova Iorque.

Formou-se na ESMAE Porto, na Indiana University Jacobs School of Music — com Virginia Zeani e Klara Barlow — e na Mason Gross School of the Arts, Rutgers University, onde concluiu o DMA em Performance Vocal.

Biography of DÓRIA

Biografia de DÓRIA

Paula Morna DÓRIA was born in Porto, Portugal. She spent nearly 8 years living in the USA, training at the Indiana University Jacobs School of Music (MM, 1998 — with Virginia Zeani & Klara Barlow) and at the Mason Gross School of the Arts, Rutgers University (DMA, 2011 — Berlioz's Les nuits d'été, FCT scholarship, grade 19/20). The DMA is registered in Portugal at Universidade de Évora.

She also holds a degree in Philosophy from the Universidade do Porto (1991). In 2003 she won the IBLA Grand Prize in Ragusa, Sicily, leading to her debut at Carnegie Weill Recital Hall, New York, 2004, and representation by a New York agent with listing in Musical America.

Paula Morna DÓRIA nasceu no Porto, Portugal. Viveu quase 8 anos nos EUA, formando-se na Indiana University Jacobs School of Music (MM, 1998 — com Virginia Zeani e Klara Barlow) e na Mason Gross School of the Arts, Rutgers University (DMA, 2011 — Les nuits d'été de Berlioz, bolsa FCT, 19/20). O DMA está registado na Universidade de Évora.

É também licenciada em Filosofia pela Universidade do Porto (1991). Em 2003 venceu o IBLA Grand Prize em Ragusa, Sicília, o que conduziu à estreia no Carnegie Weill Recital Hall, Nova Iorque, em 2004 e à representação por agente nova-iorquino com presença no Musical America.
